title: singaporandia macrophylla
intro: Singaporandia macrophylla, also known as the Singapore tree or Singapore mahogany, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Singaporandia macrophylla include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to make ointments and creams for skin and wound care.
